title: Install dependencies and verify GPU
description: Set up the Python environment with all required packages and verify CUDA availability on the A6000 GPU.

steps.step1: Install core packages: pip install torch torchvision numpy scipy scikit-learn matplotlib seaborn pandas tqdm
steps.step2: Verify GPU availability: run python -c "import torch; print(torch.cuda.is_available()); print(torch.cuda.get_device_name(0))" and confirm A6000 is detected
steps.step3: Create project directory structure: exp/ (experiment runners), exp/shared/ (shared modules), figures/, results/
steps.step4: Verify all imports work correctly with a quick smoke test

title: Download and prepare all datasets
description: Download CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100 (with superclass labels), and prepare data loaders with standard augmentation. CIFAR-100-C and Tiny-ImageNet are Tier 2 and prepared only if time allows.

steps.step1: Download CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 via torchvision.datasets (automatic download to ./data/). CIFAR-100 has 100 fine classes in 20 superclasses (coarse labels available via coarse=True).
steps.step2: Define standard training augmentation for 32x32 images: RandomResizedCrop(32, scale=(0.2, 1.0)), RandomHorizontalFlip(), ColorJitter(0.4, 0.4, 0.4, 0.1) with prob 0.8, RandomGrayscale(p=0.2), Normalize((0.5071, 0.4867, 0.4408), (0.2675, 0.2565, 0.2761)) for CIFAR-100.
steps.step3: Define test transform: Normalize only (no augmentation) with same mean/std.
steps.step4: Create a shared data_loader.py module that returns train/test DataLoaders for CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 with batch_size=512, num_workers=4, pin_memory=True. For contrastive learning, each sample returns two augmented views (TwoCropTransform wrapper).
steps.step5: Build CIFAR-100 superclass mapping: map each of 100 fine classes to its superclass (0-19). Store as a dictionary for evaluation.
steps.step6: Verify data loading: print dataset sizes (CIFAR-100: 50000 train, 10000 test), number of classes, sample shapes.
steps.step7: [Tier 2] Download Tiny-ImageNet (200 classes, 64x64) and create data loaders with RandomResizedCrop(64). Download CIFAR-100-C for corruption robustness evaluation.

title: Build shared training and evaluation utilities
description: Create reusable modules for models, losses, training loops, and evaluation that all experiments will share.

steps.step1: Create exp/shared/models.py: ResNet-18 encoder with a 2-layer MLP projection head (2048->2048->128) following SupCon protocol. Output is L2-normalized 128-dim embeddings. Also support ResNet-50 for Tier 2. Use torchvision.models.resnet18/resnet50 modified for 32x32 input (replace first 7x7 conv with 3x3 conv, remove first maxpool).
steps.step2: Create exp/shared/losses.py with: (a) SupConLoss: standard supervised contrastive loss with fixed temperature tau=0.07, (b) CGALoss: the Confusion-Geometric Alignment loss operating on class prototypes with target similarity s*(i,j) = -1/(C-1) - alpha*(c_norm(i,j) - 1/(C-1)), weighted by c_norm(i,j), (c) SupConAdaptiveTempLoss: SupCon with class-pair-specific temperatures tau(i,j) = tau_base / (1 + gamma * c_norm(i,j)), (d) HardNegLoss: hard negative mining loss following Robinson et al. (2021) with concentration parameter beta=1.0, (e) CrossEntropyLoss: standard CE baseline.
steps.step3: Create exp/shared/utils.py with: (a) ConfusionTracker: maintains EMA confusion matrix (mu=0.99), computes symmetrized normalized confusion c_norm(i,j), (b) PrototypeTracker: maintains EMA of L2-normalized class prototypes (nu=0.99), (c) LinearEvaluator: trains a linear classifier on frozen embeddings (SGD, lr=0.1, cosine schedule, 100 epochs, batch 256), (d) seed_everything(seed): sets all random seeds for reproducibility, (e) compute_metrics(): computes top-1, top-5 accuracy, within-superclass accuracy, between-superclass accuracy, ETF deviation (variance of pairwise cosine similarities of class means), hierarchy correlation (Spearman rho between pairwise distances and superclass co-membership).
steps.step4: Create exp/shared/trainer.py: generic training loop that accepts a loss function, optional ConfusionTracker and PrototypeTracker, trains for specified epochs with SGD (lr=0.5, cosine schedule, momentum=0.9, weight_decay=1e-4), batch_size=512, warmup for first 5 epochs with lambda=0 for CGA methods. Saves checkpoints and logs loss/accuracy per epoch. For CG-SupCon: ramp lambda from 0 to lambda_max over epochs 5-10.
steps.step5: Implement a results saving utility: each experiment saves results.json with {method, dataset, seed, top1_acc, top5_acc, superclass_metrics, training_time, hyperparameters}.

title: Cross-Entropy baseline on CIFAR-100
description: Train standard cross-entropy classifier as the simplest baseline. ResNet-18, 200 epochs, 5 seeds.

steps.step1: Create exp/ce_baseline/run.py: Train ResNet-18 with cross-entropy loss on CIFAR-100 for 200 epochs. SGD optimizer, lr=0.5 with cosine annealing, momentum=0.9, weight_decay=1e-4, batch_size=512. Standard augmentation.
steps.step2: Run with seeds [42, 43, 44, 45, 46]. Each run ~6 min (total ~30 min for 5 seeds sequential on 1 GPU).
steps.step3: Evaluate: top-1 accuracy, top-5 accuracy, within-superclass accuracy, between-superclass accuracy. Extract class embeddings (penultimate layer) for geometry analysis.
steps.step4: Save results to exp/ce_baseline/results_seed{N}.json. Expected: ~76-78% top-1 accuracy.
steps.step5: Estimated time: ~30 minutes total.

title: SupCon baseline on CIFAR-100
description: Train standard SupCon (Khosla et al., 2020) as the primary comparison baseline. This is the CGA-free version. ResNet-18, 200 epochs contrastive + 100 epochs linear eval, 5 seeds.

steps.step1: Create exp/supcon_baseline/run.py: Train ResNet-18 encoder with SupCon loss (tau=0.07) on CIFAR-100 for 200 epochs. SGD, lr=0.5, cosine schedule, momentum=0.9, weight_decay=1e-4, batch_size=512. Two-crop augmentation.
steps.step2: After contrastive training, freeze encoder and train linear classifier for 100 epochs (SGD, lr=0.1, cosine schedule).
steps.step3: Run with seeds [42, 43, 44, 45, 46]. Each run ~7 min contrastive + ~3 min linear = ~10 min (total ~50 min for 5 seeds).
steps.step4: Evaluate: top-1, top-5, within-superclass, between-superclass accuracy. Compute ETF deviation and hierarchy correlation from class mean embeddings.
steps.step5: Save results to exp/supcon_baseline/results_seed{N}.json. Expected: ~72-74% top-1 accuracy (ResNet-18, 200 epochs).

title: HardNeg-CL baseline on CIFAR-100
description: Train SupCon with hard negative mining (Robinson et al., 2021) as instance-level baseline. Tests class-level vs instance-level approaches.

steps.step1: Create exp/hardneg_baseline/run.py: Implement HardNeg-CL following Robinson et al. (2021). Use concentration parameter beta=1.0 (their recommended default). The loss upweights negatives that are closer to the anchor in embedding space.
steps.step2: Same training protocol as SupCon: ResNet-18, 200 epochs, SGD lr=0.5, cosine, batch_size=512, tau=0.07. Then 100 epochs linear eval.
steps.step3: Run with seeds [42, 43, 44, 45, 46]. ~10 min per seed, ~50 min total.
steps.step4: Evaluate same metrics as SupCon baseline.
steps.step5: Save results to exp/hardneg_baseline/results_seed{N}.json. Expected: ~73-75% top-1 (slight improvement over SupCon).

title: TCL baseline on CIFAR-100
description: Train Tuned Contrastive Learning (Animesh & Chandraker, 2025) as a second instance-level baseline. 3 seeds.

steps.step1: Create exp/tcl_baseline/run.py: Implement TCL with learnable gradient response parameters for hard positives (rho_pos) and negatives (rho_neg). Initialize rho_pos=rho_neg=1.0, learn via SGD with lr=0.01.
steps.step2: Same backbone training protocol: ResNet-18, 200 epochs, SGD lr=0.5, cosine, batch_size=512. Then 100 epochs linear eval.
steps.step3: Run with seeds [42, 43, 44]. ~10 min per seed, ~30 min total.
steps.step4: Evaluate same metrics.
steps.step5: Save results to exp/tcl_baseline/results_seed{N}.json. Expected: ~73-75% top-1.

title: SupCon + Pairwise Reweighting (MACL-style) baseline on CIFAR-100
description: Adapt MACL's pairwise reweighting approach using confusion frequency. Tests whether simple reweighting suffices vs geometric alignment.

steps.step1: Create exp/reweight_baseline/run.py: Standard SupCon but weight each negative pair (i,j) by w(i,j) = 1 + beta_rw * c_norm(i,j) where c_norm is from the same EMA confusion tracker used by CG-SupCon. beta_rw=2.0. This upweights confused pairs in the contrastive loss without prescribing target geometry.
steps.step2: Same training protocol: ResNet-18, 200 epochs, SGD lr=0.5, cosine, batch_size=512. Uses ConfusionTracker from shared utils. 5 epoch warmup before enabling reweighting.
steps.step3: Run with seeds [42, 43, 44]. ~10 min per seed, ~30 min total.
steps.step4: Evaluate same metrics.
steps.step5: Save results to exp/reweight_baseline/results_seed{N}.json.

title: VarCon-T baseline on CIFAR-100
description: Implement VarCon's confidence-adaptive temperature mechanism (without full ELBO). Tests per-sample temperature adaptation.

steps.step1: Create exp/varcon_t_baseline/run.py: SupCon with per-sample adaptive temperature: for anchor x, tau(x) = tau_base / (1 + gamma_v * (1 - max_c p(c|x))) where p(c|x) is the model's softmax prediction. Low-confidence samples get lower temperature (sharper). gamma_v=2.0.
steps.step2: Requires a classification head during contrastive training to compute p(c|x). Add a linear classification head trained jointly (with stop-gradient on encoder for this head, or detached). Alternatively, use nearest-prototype classification for p(c|x).
steps.step3: Same backbone protocol: ResNet-18, 200 epochs, batch_size=512. Then 100 epochs linear eval.
steps.step4: Run with seeds [42, 43, 44]. ~12 min per seed (slightly more compute for adaptive temp), ~36 min total.
steps.step5: Save results to exp/varcon_t_baseline/results_seed{N}.json.

title: CG-SupCon hyperparameter grid search on CIFAR-100
description: Run alpha/lambda grid search on CIFAR-100 with 1 seed to select best hyperparameters before the full 5-seed evaluation.

steps.step1: Create exp/cgsc_grid/run.py: CG-SupCon with grid over alpha in {0.3, 0.5, 0.7} and lambda in {0.1, 0.5, 1.0}. Fixed gamma=0 (no adaptive temp) to isolate CGA effect. Total: 9 configurations.
steps.step2: Training: ResNet-18, 200 epochs, SGD lr=0.5, cosine, batch_size=512. 5-epoch warmup (lambda=0), then lambda ramp-up over epochs 5-10. ConfusionTracker (mu=0.99) and PrototypeTracker (nu=0.99).
steps.step3: Run all 9 configs with seed=42. Each ~10 min, total ~90 min sequential.
steps.step4: Select best (alpha, lambda) by top-1 accuracy on test set. Record all results for sensitivity analysis figure.
steps.step5: Save results to exp/cgsc_grid/results_alpha{A}_lambda{L}.json.

title: CG-SupCon full evaluation on CIFAR-100
description: Run CG-SupCon with best hyperparameters from grid search, 5 seeds, full metric evaluation.

steps.step1: Create exp/cgsc_main/run.py: CG-SupCon with best (alpha, lambda) from grid search. gamma=0 for CGA-only variant; also run with gamma=5 for full CG-SupCon.
steps.step2: Run CGA-only variant (gamma=0) with seeds [42, 43, 44, 45, 46]. ~10 min per seed, ~50 min total.
steps.step3: Run full CG-SupCon (gamma=5) with seeds [42, 43, 44, 45, 46]. ~12 min per seed, ~60 min total.
steps.step4: Full evaluation for each seed: top-1, top-5, within-superclass accuracy, between-superclass accuracy, ETF deviation, hierarchy correlation (Spearman rho), top-20 confused pair reduction, training time per epoch.
steps.step5: Save results to exp/cgsc_main/results_cga_seed{N}.json and exp/cgsc_main/results_full_seed{N}.json.

title: CGA component ablation study
description: Ablate each component of CG-SupCon to validate that CGA is the primary contributor. 4 variants, 3 seeds each.

steps.step1: Create exp/ablation_components/run.py with 4 variants: (A) SupCon only (lambda=0, gamma=0) -- reuses SupCon baseline results, (B) SupCon + CGA only (best lambda, gamma=0) -- reuses CGA-only from main experiment, (C) SupCon + adaptive temp only (lambda=0, gamma=5), (D) CG-SupCon full (best lambda, gamma=5) -- reuses full CG-SupCon from main experiment.
steps.step2: Only need to run variant C (adaptive temp only) freshly with seeds [42, 43, 44]. ~12 min each, 3 runs = ~36 min.
steps.step3: Key test: variant B (CGA only) should provide the majority of gain over A (SupCon). Variant D should be >= B. Variant C (adaptive temp only) should provide smaller gain than B.
steps.step4: Report delta accuracy for each component: CGA contribution = B - A, temp contribution = D - B, interaction = D - (B + C - A).
steps.step5: Save results to exp/ablation_components/results_variant{X}_seed{N}.json.
steps.step6: Estimated time: ~36 minutes (only variant C needs fresh runs).

title: Alpha and lambda sensitivity analysis
description: Analyze sensitivity of CG-SupCon to CGA strength (alpha) and regularizer weight (lambda). Extends grid search with boundary values.

steps.step1: Use results from the alpha/lambda grid search (9 configurations, 1 seed each). Additionally run alpha in {0.1, 1.0} with best lambda, and lambda in {0.01, 5.0} with best alpha, each with seed=42. Total: 4 additional runs.
steps.step2: Create exp/sensitivity/run.py for the additional boundary configurations.
steps.step3: Plot heatmap: alpha (x-axis) vs lambda (y-axis), color = top-1 accuracy. Show that performance is robust across a range.
steps.step4: Plot 1D sensitivity curves: accuracy vs alpha (fixing best lambda), accuracy vs lambda (fixing best alpha).
steps.step5: Save results to exp/sensitivity/results_alpha{A}_lambda{L}.json.
steps.step6: Estimated time: ~40 minutes total (4 additional runs).

title: Wall-clock overhead measurement
description: Measure training time per epoch for each method to demonstrate CGA has negligible overhead.

steps.step1: For each method (CE, SupCon, HardNeg-CL, CG-SupCon), time 10 training epochs on CIFAR-100 with ResNet-18, batch_size=512. Use torch.cuda.synchronize() before timing.
steps.step2: Compute mean and std of time per epoch across the 10 epochs (discard first epoch as warmup).
steps.step3: Report overhead as percentage increase over SupCon baseline. Target: CG-SupCon overhead < 10% (expected < 5% given O(C^2) CGA cost).
steps.step4: Save timing results to exp/overhead/timing_results.json.
steps.step5: Estimated time: ~15 minutes total.

title: Embedding geometry and confusion analysis
description: Analyze the learned embedding geometry to validate that CGA breaks ETF symmetry in a confusion-structured way.

steps.step1: For SupCon and CG-SupCon (best seed): extract class mean embeddings from the test set (average L2-normalized embeddings per class). Compute pairwise cosine similarity matrix (100x100 for CIFAR-100).
steps.step2: Compute ETF deviation: variance of off-diagonal pairwise cosine similarities. SupCon should have low variance (near-ETF). CG-SupCon should have higher variance (broken ETF).
steps.step3: Compute hierarchy correlation: Spearman rank correlation between pairwise embedding distances and ground-truth superclass co-membership (binary: 1 if same superclass, 0 otherwise). CG-SupCon should have higher correlation (Spearman rho improvement > 0.1 over SupCon).
steps.step4: Compute confusion reduction: for the top-20 most confused class pairs (from SupCon), measure the reduction in confusion values in CG-SupCon.
steps.step5: Verify Theorem 1 empirically: check that inter-class cosine similarities are monotonically ordered by confusion strength. Compute Spearman correlation between confusion values c(i,j) and cosine similarities p_i.p_j. Should be strongly negative for CG-SupCon.
steps.step7: Estimated time: ~10 minutes (analysis on saved embeddings, no training).

title: [Tier 2] Few-shot evaluation
description: Evaluate frozen embeddings on few-shot classification tasks. Tests whether structured geometry improves low-data generalization.

steps.step1: Use the full CIFAR-100-trained encoders from SupCon and CG-SupCon. Sample 5-way tasks from all 100 classes.
steps.step2: For each task: sample 5 classes, k support samples per class (k=1 and k=5), 15 query samples per class. Use nearest-centroid classification on L2-normalized embeddings.
steps.step3: Run 600 episodes for each setting (5-way 1-shot, 5-way 5-shot) with seeds [42, 43, 44]. Report mean accuracy and 95% CI.
steps.step4: Compare SupCon vs CG-SupCon. CG-SupCon should show 2-5% improvement since structured geometry helps when data is scarce.
steps.step5: Save results to exp/fewshot/results_seed{N}.json.
steps.step6: Estimated time: ~10 minutes total (no training, just evaluation).

title: [Tier 2] CIFAR-100-C corruption robustness
description: Evaluate on corrupted test images to test robustness of learned representations.

steps.step1: Download CIFAR-100-C (15 corruption types x 5 severity levels). If not available via standard download, generate corruptions using torchvision transforms or the imagecorruptions library.
steps.step2: For SupCon and CG-SupCon (seed=42): evaluate the linear classifier on each corruption at severity 5 (most severe). Report per-corruption and mean accuracy.
steps.step3: Compute mean Corruption Error (mCE) relative to CE baseline following standard protocol.
steps.step4: CG-SupCon should show 1-3% improvement in mean corruption accuracy due to more structured geometry.
steps.step5: Save results to exp/corruption/results.json.
steps.step6: Estimated time: ~20 minutes (evaluation only, no training).

title: [Tier 2] Stability analysis: class-pair vs instance-level
description: Validate that class-pair confusion is more stable than instance-level hardness, supporting the theoretical motivation.

steps.step1: Using CG-SupCon checkpoints at epochs 50, 100, 150, 200 for 3 seeds: extract (a) top-20 most confused class pairs from confusion matrix, (b) top-20 hardest negative instances (by embedding distance to anchor).
steps.step2: Compute Kendall tau rank correlation of top-20 rankings between: (a) different epochs same seed, (b) different seeds same epoch.
steps.step3: Report mean Kendall tau for class-pair rankings (expected > 0.7) and instance-level rankings (expected < 0.4).
steps.step4: This validates that class-pair confusion provides a more stable signal for geometric alignment.
steps.step5: Save results to exp/stability/results.json.
steps.step6: Estimated time: ~10 minutes (analysis on saved checkpoints).

title: [Tier 2] Tiny-ImageNet evaluation
description: Test scalability to 200 classes on Tiny-ImageNet.

steps.step1: Download Tiny-ImageNet (200 classes, 100K train, 10K val, 64x64). Create data loaders with augmentation adapted for 64x64 (RandomResizedCrop(64)).
steps.step2: Train SupCon and CG-SupCon with ResNet-18 for 100 epochs each, seed=42. Same optimizer settings but adjust lr schedule for 100 epochs.
steps.step3: Linear eval for 100 epochs. Evaluate top-1, top-5 accuracy.
steps.step4: Save results to exp/tinyimagenet/results_{method}_seed42.json.
steps.step5: Estimated time: ~40 minutes total (2 runs x 20 min each).

title: [Tier 2] CIFAR-10 sanity check
description: Verify the method works on a simpler dataset. With only 10 classes, the benefit should be smaller but still present.

steps.step1: Train CE, SupCon, and CG-SupCon on CIFAR-10 with ResNet-18, 200 epochs, seeds [42, 43, 44].
steps.step3: Report top-1 accuracy. Gains should be smaller (0.5-1%) since only 10 classes means less inter-class confusion structure to exploit.
steps.step4: Save results to exp/cifar10/results_{method}_seed{N}.json.
steps.step5: Estimated time: ~30 minutes total (9 runs x ~3 min each).

title: Statistical testing and success criteria evaluation
description: Aggregate all results, compute statistics, and evaluate against the 8 success criteria.

steps.step1: Create aggregate_results.py that reads all results JSON files and computes: mean +/- std across seeds for each method and metric.
steps.step2: Statistical tests: paired t-test (5 seeds) between CG-SupCon and each baseline on CIFAR-100 top-1 accuracy. Report p-values. Success criterion 1: p < 0.05 vs SupCon.
steps.step3: Evaluate success criterion 2: CG-SupCon > HardNeg-CL and TCL on CIFAR-100 top-1.
steps.step4: Evaluate success criterion 3: CG-SupCon > Pairwise Reweighting on CIFAR-100 top-1.
steps.step5: Evaluate success criterion 4: CGA-only variant > SupCon (CGA is primary contributor).
steps.step6: Evaluate success criterion 5: within-superclass accuracy improvement > between-superclass improvement.
steps.step7: Evaluate success criterion 6: hierarchy correlation Spearman rho improvement > 0.1 over SupCon.
steps.step8: Evaluate success criterion 7: gains hold on at least one additional dataset.
steps.step10: Save comprehensive results table to results/results.json with all metrics, p-values, and success criteria pass/fail.

title: Generate paper figures and tables
description: Create all figures and tables for the paper, covering main results, ablations, geometry analysis, and sensitivity.

steps.step1: Create figures/create_figures.py that generates all plots.
steps.step2: Table 1 (Main Results): CIFAR-100 comparison table with all methods. Columns: Method, Top-1 (mean +/- std), Top-5, Within-SC, Between-SC, ETF Dev, Hier. Corr., Time/epoch. Bold best, underline second best. Save as figures/table1_main_results.tex and .csv.
steps.step3: Figure 1 (Embedding Geometry): 2D visualization (t-SNE or PCA) of class mean embeddings for SupCon vs CG-SupCon on CIFAR-100. Color by superclass. CG-SupCon should show more superclass-clustered structure. Save as figures/figure1_geometry.pdf and .png.
steps.step4: Figure 2 (Confusion-Distance Correlation): Scatter plot of confusion c(i,j) vs cosine similarity p_i.p_j for all class pairs, separately for SupCon and CG-SupCon. CG-SupCon should show stronger negative correlation (validating Theorem 1). Save as figures/figure2_confusion_distance.pdf and .png.
steps.step5: Figure 3 (Ablation Bar Chart): Grouped bar chart showing accuracy of 4 ablation variants (SupCon, +CGA, +AdaptTemp, +Both). Shows CGA is the primary contributor. Save as figures/figure3_ablation.pdf and .png.
steps.step6: Figure 4 (Sensitivity Heatmap): Heatmap of alpha vs lambda with accuracy as color. Show robustness across hyperparameter range. Save as figures/figure4_sensitivity.pdf and .png.
steps.step7: Figure 5 (Training Dynamics): Plot CGA loss and confusion matrix entropy over training epochs. Show that confusion matrix stabilizes and CGA loss decreases. Save as figures/figure5_training_dynamics.pdf and .png.
steps.step8: Table 2 (Ablation): Detailed ablation results. Save as figures/table2_ablation.tex and .csv.
steps.step9: [Tier 2] Figure 6 (Stability Analysis): Box plot comparing Kendall tau of class-pair rankings vs instance-level rankings across seeds/epochs.
steps.step10: [Tier 2] Table 3 (Additional Datasets): Results on CIFAR-10, Tiny-ImageNet, CIFAR-100-C.

title: Runtime budget and execution order
description: Plan the sequential execution order to maximize GPU utilization within the 8-hour budget on 1x A6000.

steps.step1: Phase 1 - Baselines (~3.7h sequential): CE baseline 5 seeds (30 min), SupCon baseline 5 seeds (50 min), HardNeg-CL 5 seeds (50 min), TCL 3 seeds (30 min), Pairwise Reweight 3 seeds (30 min), VarCon-T 3 seeds (36 min).
steps.step2: Phase 2 - Grid search (~1.5h): alpha/lambda grid 9 configs (90 min). Select best hyperparameters.
steps.step3: Phase 3 - Main + Ablation (~2.5h): CG-SupCon CGA-only 5 seeds (50 min), CG-SupCon full 5 seeds (60 min), Ablation variant C 3 seeds (36 min).
steps.step4: Phase 4 - Analysis (~0.5h): Overhead measurement (15 min), Geometry analysis (10 min), Statistical tests (5 min).
steps.step5: Phase 5 - Visualization (~0.25h): Generate all figures and tables (15 min).
steps.step6: Total Tier 1: ~8h. If behind schedule, apply cutback: reduce TCL/VarCon-T to 1 seed (saves 40 min), reduce sensitivity boundary configs (saves 40 min).
steps.step7: Tier 2 experiments (if time allows, ~2h): CIFAR-10 (30 min), Few-shot eval (10 min), CIFAR-100-C (20 min), Stability analysis (10 min), Tiny-ImageNet (40 min).
steps.step8: Key optimization: reuse CGA-only and full CG-SupCon results from main experiment in ablation study (saves ~100 min of redundant training).
